description:
A compliance officer has requested a quick, script-friendly audit of a single INI-style configuration file that already exists on the machine.  
    Your job is to:

    1. Read the file  
          /home/user/config/system.cfg  

       Its exact current contents are:

          [General]
          hostname=server01
          os=ubuntu
          version=20.04

          [Security]
          firewall=enabled
          selinux=disabled
          password_policy=weak

          [Compliance]
          data_encryption=aes256
          logging=disabled


    2. Identify every key whose value is **disabled**, **weak**, or **none** (case-sensitive comparison).  
       • Only those three literal strings mark a setting as “non-compliant”.  
       • Ignore every other value.

    3. Create a plain-text log file at  
          /home/user/compliance/compliance_report.log  

       with one non-compliant setting per line, using the format

          SECTION.KEY=value

       in the precise order obtained by sorting alphabetically on “SECTION.KEY”.  
       Do not include any extra spaces or blank lines.  
       Ensure the file ends with a single trailing newline character (Unix line ending).

       Example of the exact required output format (this is only an example, not the real data):

          Security.somekey=disabled
          System.otherkey=weak
          <newline here and nothing else>

    4. If the directory /home/user/compliance does not yet exist, create it first with default user permissions (mode 755 is fine).

    Notes & Verification:
    • The automated checker will only look for the file /home/user/compliance/compliance_report.log and verify its exact byte-for-byte contents.  
    • No other files have to be modified or created.  
    • You do not need root (sudo) privileges.  
    • Bash utilities such as grep, awk, cut, sed, sort, etc. are all available.

    When finished, you can display the contents of the resulting file to the console, but that is optional.
truth:
Expected final file: /home/user/compliance/compliance_report.log

    Exact required content (including the trailing newline):

    Compliance.logging=disabled
    Security.password_policy=weak
    Security.selinux=disabled
    \n   <-- one Unix newline character (0x0A)

    No leading or trailing spaces on any line.
name: task_000000_5f2264d0
